text: Artificial intelligence is advancing quickly, and many experts believe we are moving toward a future with general AI. Unlike today’s narrow AI, which can only do specific tasks, artificial general intelligence (AGI) would be able to learn and think like a human across many different areas. This could bring huge benefits, like solving complex medical problems or managing climate change. However, there are serious risks, too. If not controlled properly, AGI could be misused or make decisions that harm people. Predictions about when we will achieve AGI vary widely. Some think it might happen within the next ten years, while others say it could take decades. The key is to develop it safely.
